scenes.script_line: "Please move the chairs" — one task, different pictures. The assumption problem.
scenes.visual_direction: Instructions given but read differently — mixed signals. Foreman pointing / worker looking puzzled.

scenes.script_line: Great teams don't just work hard — great teams communicate clearly.
scenes.visual_direction: Crew talking things through together on site — alignment, shared understanding. Group huddle.

clips.author: Uploaded
clips.note: S03 — “Great teams do not only work hard. Great teams communicate clearly.”.mp4

scenes.script_line: Communication saves time, prevents mistakes, builds trust, and people enjoy working together.
scenes.visual_direction: Two workers collaborating well — trust and camaraderie. Handshake / working side by side smiling.

scenes.script_line: The biggest mistake is that we assume — we assume we understood.
scenes.visual_direction: A worker heading off on his own assumption — quiet uncertainty. Solo builder pausing, unsure.

clips.author: Uploaded
clips.note: S05 — “The solution is simple... Clarify. Ask questions.”.mp4

scenes.script_line: The solution is simple — ask clarifying questions.
scenes.visual_direction: An apprentice asking the foreman a question, engaging — curiosity, professionalism. Pointing at the work.

clips.author: Uploaded
clips.note: S06 — “These questions are not signs of weakness. They are signs of professionalism..mp4

scenes.script_line: "What does success look like? When do you need it finished?" — checking the plan.
scenes.visual_direction: Builders reviewing the drawings/blueprint together — clarity, shared picture. Plans on site.

scenes.script_line: "Clean the kitchen" — everyone worked hard but had a different picture of the job.
scenes.visual_direction: A crew each busy on their own part of the same job — effort without alignment. Several workers, different tasks.

scenes.script_line: A two-minute conversation saves thirty minutes of confusion.
scenes.visual_direction: Two workers a quick chat by the materials / wheelbarrow — efficient, easy. Brief exchange.

scenes.script_line: Great people listen so they can understand — stop, look, listen, don't interrupt.
scenes.visual_direction: Foreman listening attentively to a worker, eye contact — attention, respect. One talking, one truly listening.

scenes.script_line: Check in — send a message, walk over: "This is my progress. Am I on track?"
scenes.visual_direction: A worker with a tablet/phone ON SITE, or foreman checking progress — staying connected.

scenes.script_line: Bad news doesn't get better with time — speak up early so people can help.
scenes.visual_direction: A worker pointing out a problem to the foreman — honesty, courage. Raising a concern, inspecting an issue together.

scenes.script_line: Because great teams don't guess — great teams communicate. Respect, trust, success.
scenes.visual_direction: Crew together, satisfied, in front of the finished work — pride, unity. Wide team + warm detail.

scenes.script_line: [S14] Good communicators notice what has NOT been said — unclear deadline, changed priority, missing information.
scenes.visual_direction: A worker reviews incomplete instructions, notices missing details, asks the manager to confirm deadline/priority/result.

scenes.script_line: [S15] A task can begin correctly and still move in the wrong direction — priorities change, new information arrives.
scenes.visual_direction: A worker progresses, receives updated information, checks with the manager, adjusts before wasting time.

scenes.script_line: [S16] Some messages should be sent immediately; some discussed face to face; some written down.
scenes.visual_direction: Sequence: urgent phone call → face-to-face discussion → written instructions confirmed by message.

scenes.script_line: [S17] The message is not complete when it is spoken — it is complete when it is UNDERSTOOD.
scenes.visual_direction: One worker gives instructions, the second repeats them back correctly, then completes the task as intended.
